title: S-measure for new radio (nr)
abstract: Embodiments of the present disclosure describe methods, apparatuses, storage media, and systems for evaluating a cell-level quality and determining whether measurements of neighbouring cells are optional or not based on the evaluation in new radio (NR) operations at millimeter wave and sub-millimeter wave frequencies, in which beamforming may be used by an access node (AN) or a user equipment (UE). Various embodiments describe how to determine wheher the measurements of non-serving cells are optional, which may reduce excessive measurements but maintain high quality wireless connections between UEs and ANs. Other embodiments may be described and claimed.
claims:
Claims Claims What is claimed is: 1. One or more computer-readable media comprising instructions to, upon execution of the instructions by one or more processors of a user equipment (UE), cause the UE to: measure a plurality of beams of a cell; determine, based on measured values of the plurality of beams, a value to indicate a cell-level quality with respect to the cell; and determine, based on a comparison between the value and an s-Measure threshold, whether measurements of neighbouring cells are optional for the UE. 2. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein the plurality of beams of the cell are formed by an access node (AN). 3. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein the instructions, when executed, further cause the UE to: identify, based on configuration information that is predefined or received from an access node (AN), a number; determine at least the number of the plurality of beams have the measured values greater than the s-Measure threshold; and determine that the measurements of neighbouring cells are optional based on said determination that at least the number of the measured plurality of beams have the measured values greater than the s-Measure threshold. 4. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein to determine the value to indicate the cell-level quality, the UE is to calculate a linear average of power levels based on the measured values of the plurality of beams. 5. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein, upon execution, the instructions are to further cause the UE to: determine a maximum measured value of the measured values of the plurality of beams; select the measured values of the plurality of beams that exceed the maximum measured value minus an offset value; and calculate a linear average of power levels based on the selected measured values. 6. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein, upon execution, the instructions are to further cause the UE to: compare individual measured values of the plurality of beams to a threshold value; and select, based on the comparisons of the individual measured values to the threshold value, one or more measured values that exceed the threshold value for the determination of the value to indicate the cell-level quality. 7. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein, upon execution, the instructions are to further cause the UE to: compare individual measured values of the plurality of beams to a threshold value; and select, based on a determination that the threshold value is greater than or equal to each of individual power levels, a maximum value of the individual measured values for the determination of the value to indicate the cell-level quality. 8. The one or more computer-readable media of any one of claims 1-4, wherein, upon execution, the instructions are to further cause the UE to: compare the value to the s-Measure threshold; determine the value exceeds the s-Measure threshold; and determine, based on the determination that the value exceeds the s-Measure threshold, that the measurements of neighbouring cells are optional. 9. The one or more computer-readable media of any one of claims 1, 2, and 4, wherein, upon execution, the instructions are to further cause the UE to: identify, based on configuration information that is predefined or received from an access node (AN), a number; and select the number of the plurality of beams that have the measured values greater than theother measured values for the determination of the value to indicate the cell-level quality. 10. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein the measurements with respect to the neighbouring cells are measurements of intra-frequency, inter- frequency, or inter-radio access technology (inter-RAT) neighbouring cells. 11. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1 or 4, wherein the measurements of the plurality of beams are to measure reference signal received power (RSRP) of the plurality of beams respectively to obtain a plurality of RSRP values. 12. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein the plurality of beams include a channel-state information reference signal (CSI-RS). 13. One or more computer-readable media comprising instructions to, upon execution of the instructions by one or more processors of an access node (AN), cause the AN to: generate a plurality of beams of a cell; transmit the plurality of beams so that a user equipment (UE) is to measure the plurality of beams based on reception by the UE; and configure the UE with an s-Measure threshold to determine an optionality of measurements of neighbouring cells for the UE based on one or more comparisons with the s-Measure threshold. 14. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 13, wherein, upon execution, the instructions are to further cause the AN to configure a number for the UE so that at least the number of UE measured beams have measured values greater than the s-Measure threshold. 15. An apparatus of a user equipment (UE), comprising: one or more baseband processors to measure a plurality of power levels of respective beams of a cell; and a central processing unit (CPU) coupled with the one or more baseband processors, the CPU to: acquire an s-Measure threshold, and compare one or more of the plurality of power levels, or a value to indicate a cell-level quality with respect to the cell based on the plurality of power levels, to 

 the s-Measure threshold to determine whether that measurements of neighbouring cells are optional. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the CPU is further to: determine, based on the one or more of the plurality of power levels, the value to indicate the cell-level quality with respect to the cell; compare the value to the s-Measure threshold; and determine, based on the comparison between the value and the s-Measure threshold, whether that the measurements of neighbouring cells are optional. 17. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein to determine the value to indicate the cell-level quality, the CPU is to calculate a linear average of the one or more of the plurality of power levels. 18. The apparatus of claim 16 or 17, wherein the CPU is further to: identify, based on configuration information that is predefined or received from an access node (AN), a number, and determine, based on the comparisons between the one or more of the plurality of power levels and the s-Measure threshold, that at least the number of the compared power levels exceed the s-Measure threshold. 19. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the CPU is further to: compare the one or more of the plurality of power levels to the s-Measure threshold, determine, based on the comparisons between the one or more of the plurality of power levels and the s-Measure threshold, that at least one of the compared power levels exceeds the s-Measure threshold, and determine that measurements of neighbouring cells are optional, based on the determination that at least one of the compared power levels exceeds the s-Measure threshold. 20. The apparatus of claim 19, wherein the CPU is further to: identify, based on configuration information that is predefined or received from an access node (AN), a number, 

 determine at least the number of the compared power levels exceed the s-Measure threshold, and determine that measurements of neighbouring cells are optional, based on the determination that at least the number of the compared power levels exceeds the s- Measure threshold. 21. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the CPU is further to: select, based on comparing the plurality of power levels to a threshold value, power levels of the plurality of power levels that exceed the threshold value, determine, based on linear averaging the selected power levels, the value to indicate the cell-level quality with respect to the cell, and determine, based on the comparison between the value and the s-Measure threshold, that measurements of neighbouring cells are optional. 22. The apparatus of any one of claims 15-21 , wherein the measurements of the plurality of power levels are to measure reference signal received power (RSRP) of the plurality of beams respectively to obtain a plurality of RSRP power levels. 23. The apparatus of any one of claims 16-21 , wherein the measurements with respect to the neighbouring cells are measurements of intra-frequency, inter-frequency, or inter- radio access technology (inter-RAT) neighbouring cells. 24. An apparatus of baseband circuitry of an access node (AN), comprises: means for generating a plurality of beams to include a reference signal; means for transmitting the plurality of beams to a user equipment (UE); and means for configuring the UE with an s-Measure threshold to determine an optionality of measurements of neighbouring cells for the UE based on one or more comparisons with the s-Measure threshold. 25. The apparatus of claim 24, wherein the reference signal includes a channel-state information reference signal (CSI-RS).
